breaking.text: S&P 500 futures slip 0.4% premarket as 15% global tariff expected to take effect this week
Iran denies peace talk reports as 'psychological warfare'; marine war-risk insurance canceled for Strait of Hormuz effective today
Broadcom guides Q2 revenue to $22B, smashing $20.4B consensus; sees AI chip revenue above $100B in 2027
S&P 500 futures down ~0.4% as markets weigh 15% global tariff implementation and Strait of Hormuz disruptions
Kroger Q4 earnings beat on EPS ($1.28 vs $1.20 est) but misses on revenue; issues soft 2026 guidance

summary: S&P 500 futures are down about 0.4% in premarket trading on Thursday after the index rallied 0.8% to 6,869 on Wednesday, led by a 1.3% Nasdaq surge as tech and semiconductor stocks dominated. Broadcom delivered a blockbuster Q1 report after the bell — revenue jumped 29% to $19.3B with AI revenue doubling to $8.4B — and stunned markets with Q2 guidance of $22B and a $100B+ AI chip revenue target for 2027, sending shares up 5% after hours. Geopolitical crosscurrents persist as Iran officially denied reports of back-channel peace talks with the US, calling them 'psychological warfare,' while marine insurers canceled war-risk coverage for the Strait of Hormuz effective March 5, keeping Brent crude elevated near $84 with a $10-15 risk premium embedded. Adding to uncertainty, Treasury Secretary Bessent confirmed the global tariff rate will rise from 10% to 15% this week under Section 122 authority, with plans to return to prior levels within five months as formal trade reviews proceed.

highlights.text: Marvell Technology reports Q4 earnings after today's close — analysts expect EPS of $0.79 on $2.21B revenue amid surging AI networking demand
Iran's intelligence ministry calls NYT report of back-channel CIA peace outreach 'pure falsehood and psychological warfare,' dashing hopes for near-term de-escalation
ADP private payrolls beat expectations with 63K jobs added in February vs 48K consensus, rebounding sharply from January's revised 11K — weekly jobless claims due this morning
Marine insurers Gard and Skuld issue 'Notice of Cancellation' for war-risk coverage in the Strait of Hormuz effective March 5, further disrupting global energy trade flows

Broadcom CEO Hock Tan projects AI chip revenue 'significantly in excess of $100 billion' in 2027, naming Google, Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI among six key custom chip customers — Q2 guidance of $22B represents 47% YoY growth
Strait of Hormuz tanker insurance pulled for March 5, effectively halting all shipping traffic; Brent crude holds near $82-84 as White House offers no timeline for safe passage
S&P 500 futures slip 0.4% premarket as Polymarket shows 70% odds of a down open; weekly jobless claims and productivity data due at 8:30 AM ahead of Friday's critical payrolls report
BJ's Wholesale beats on Q4 EPS at $1.11 but misses on revenue at $5.36B vs $5.39B expected, mirroring the mixed retail earnings theme this morning
Ciena Corp crushes Q4 estimates with EPS of $1.35 vs $1.16 expected and revenue of $1.43B (up 33% YoY), driven by surging AI infrastructure demand

Kroger Q4 adjusted EPS beats at $1.28 vs $1.20 expected, but revenue misses at $34.73B vs $35B est; eCommerce sales surged 20% YoY — stock dips ~1% premarket on soft 2026 guidance of 1-2% identical sales growth
Geopolitical tensions ease slightly as reports indicate Iranian operatives reached out to U.S. to explore potential peace talks
Trump's 15% global tariff expected to take effect this week per Treasury Secretary Bessent, up from current 10% rate under Trade Act Section 122
Costco reports Q2 earnings after close today; consensus expects $4.55 EPS on $69.3B revenue with strong membership income growth
Marvell Technology reports Q4 earnings today with analysts expecting $0.79 EPS on $2.2B revenue; options traders pricing in 11% move on AI chip demand
S&P 500 futures down 1.5% and Nasdaq futures off 2% in premarket as Iran vows to escalate retaliation, pushing Brent crude toward $84/barrel
Ross Stores surges 7.4% after Q4 comp sales jump 9% (double estimates), EPS of $2.00 beats guidance; plans 110 new stores in 2026
EU expects to be exempt from U.S. universal tariff hike to 15%, keeping the bloc at 10% under existing trade deal framework
CoreWeave lands multi-year deal to power Perplexity's AI inference on Nvidia GB200 NVL72 clusters, shares pop 8%
Okta beats Q4 with $0.90 EPS vs $0.85 expected and $761M revenue, but stock falls after-hours as FY27 guidance implies growth deceleration to ~9%
Broadcom Q2 revenue guidance of $22B crushes Street estimate of $20.6B; announces $10B share buyback and projects $100B+ AI chip revenue by 2027
